_comment: Epic N (ADR 0035) N-S3 — C++ namespace + `using` + cross-file namespace resolution on the tree-sitter floor (ceiling `likely`). A function inside `namespace ns { }` is qualified `ns::f` (decl pass treats the namespace as an enclosing scope, like a class in N-S1), accumulating through nesting (`outer::inner::deep`). A bare call from WITHIN the namespace resolves enclosing-first: `side()` inside `geometry::area` -> `geometry::side`. A SCOPED call binds to the declaration whose node id is the call's FULL `::`-path: `compute -> geometry::area`, `main -> geometry::side`, and `useDeep -> outer::inner::deep` (NOT the unrelated `inner::deep` of `struct inner` — full-path match, no 2-segment collision). A scoped call whose target is NOT in this file binds CROSS-FILE to the unique same-directory namespace-member definition: `useRemote -> lib.cpp#remote::fetch` (the N-S2 dir-as-module proxy extended to namespace members; class members are NOT indexed; ambiguous/cross-dir stays `unresolved`). Only functions whose qualified name comes from their ENCLOSING scope are indexed — an OUT-OF-LINE member definition (`int Gadget::spin(){}` at file scope) is NOT a cross-file target, so `useGadget`'s `Gadget::spin()` stays `unresolved` (conservative: never over-resolve a member call). CROSS-DIRECTORY: a same-dir miss binds via the `#include` graph — `ns.cpp` `#include "sub/deep.h"`, whose sibling source `sub/deep.cpp` defines `farns::compute`, so `useFar -> sub/deep.cpp#farns::compute` resolves across the `sub/` boundary (only when exactly one include-connected file defines the name). `using` declarations resolve bare calls: `using geometry::side;` makes `viaUsing`'s bare `side()` -> `geometry::side`, and `using namespace text;` makes bare `helper()` -> `text::helper` (file-scoped approximation; bound only when the member is declared in-file; >1 used namespace declaring a name stays `unresolved`). CONFLICTING using-declarations (`using one::dup;` + `using two::dup;`) are AMBIGUOUS, so `useDup`'s bare `dup()` stays `unresolved` — never a guess. `text::helper` is unreachable from `main`. An unknown scope/name stays `unresolved`. OVER-RESOLUTION regressions (`edges.cpp`, from the ADR 0035 adversarial audit — a false edge is worse than `unresolved`): a `.`/`->` member call (`conn->send()`, `conn.send()`) stays `unresolved`, never binds to a same-named free function; a constructor `Box()` is NOT a call edge to the `Box` class node; an explicit-global `::glob()` binds to the free `glob`, never the same-named member `Holder::glob`.
